rev: A long-running, affordable recreational gym across pools and community clubs; strong on playful coordination rather than competitive polish.
Solid mall-based recreational gym with holiday camps and parties; pricing isn't published so you'll need to enquire.
A well-known franchise with four outlets and structured age-banded classes; great energy but franchise pricing adds up.
A Holland Village mainstay with a clear progression from Kindergym into recreational and competitive tracks.
Polished Orchard-based programme with a clear curriculum; premium positioning means it's pricier than community-club options.
Bukit Timah academy with both recreational and serious competitive pathways, suiting kids who may want to progress.
East-side gym offering cheap $15 trial classes, a low-risk way to test if your child takes to it.
Runs out of HomeTeamNS and SAFRA clubhouses and notably caters to special-needs gymnasts too.
Toa Payoh club leaning toward artistic gymnastics; the per-term fee is on the higher side for casual learners.
Multiple outlets combining gymnastics, parkour and trampoline; a good fit for kids who like to climb and tumble freely.
One of the few centres focused on rhythmic gymnastics, blending dance-like artistry with athletic discipline.
Based at Orchid Country Club with a clear beginner-to-advanced ladder; location may be a stretch for non-northeast families.
Covers both artistic and rhythmic gymnastics across community venues, though the youngest classes start at five.
A physio-designed, non-competitive programme teaching ten sports across 50+ preschools, ideal for building broad movement basics under age six.
Tampines Hub gym with discounted trial classes; the term commitment of 11 lessons plus a registration fee is worth checking before signing.
Preschool-focused multi-sport classes built around fundamental movement skills before introducing specific sports.
Play-based multi-sport sessions emphasising character and fun over competition, suited to younger or hesitant kids.
